(1) Notwithstanding the provisions of any other law to the contrary, every driver of a vehicle shall exercise due care to avoid colliding with any horse being ridden or led along a public highway.

(2) Every driver of a vehicle shall approach a horse being ridden or led along a public highway at a reasonable and prudent speed so as to avoid frightening such horse and shall pass the horse at a reasonable distance.

(3) No driver of a vehicle shall sound the horn when approaching or passing a horse on a public highway.
markdown:
# Vehicle & Traffic Law
## Title 7
#### Article 26. Right of Way
##### § 1146-A — Approaching horses

(1) Notwithstanding the provisions of any other law to the contrary, every driver of a vehicle shall exercise due care to avoid colliding with any horse being ridden or led along a public highway.

(2) Every driver of a vehicle shall approach a horse being ridden or led along a public highway at a reasonable and prudent speed so as to avoid frightening such horse and shall pass the horse at a reasonable distance.

(3) No driver of a vehicle shall sound the horn when approaching or passing a horse on a public highway.

*Last modified: Sep. 22, 2014*

subsections.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of any other law to the contrary, every driver of a vehicle shall exercise due care to avoid colliding with any horse being ridden or led along a public highway.

Every driver of a vehicle shall approach a horse being ridden or led along a public highway at a reasonable and prudent speed so as to avoid frightening such horse and shall pass the horse at a reasonable distance.
No driver of a vehicle shall sound the horn when approaching or passing a horse on a public highway.
